Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is on the Signup screen of the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), valid password (e.g., "Password123!"), valid name (e.g., "John Doe"), valid phone number (e.g., "+393123456789")
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid email address in the email field.
2. Enter valid password in the password field.
3. Re-enter the same password in the confirm password field.
4. Enter valid name in the name field.
5. Enter valid phone number in the phone number field.
6. Click on the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the Login screen.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User is on the Signup screen of the app.
Test Data: Invalid email (e.g., "user@example"), valid password (e.g., "Password123!"), valid name (e.g., "John Doe"), valid phone number (e.g., "+393123456789")
Test Steps:
1. Enter invalid email address in the email field.
2. Enter valid password in the password field.
3. Re-enter the same password in the confirm password field.
4. Enter valid name in the name field.
5. Enter valid phone number in the phone number field.
6. Click on the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid. User remains on the Signup screen.

Title: User Registration with Mismatched Passwords
Preconditions: User is on the Signup screen of the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., "user@example.com"), first password (e.g., "Password123!"), second password (e.g., "Password456!"), valid name (e.g., "John Doe"), valid phone number (e.g., "+393123456789")
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid email address in the email field.
2. Enter first password in the password field.
3. Enter second password (different from the first) in the confirm password field.
4. Enter valid name in the name field.
5. Enter valid phone number in the phone number field.
6. Click on the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating the passwords do not match. User remains on the Signup screen.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has a registered account and is on the Login screen of the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., "user@example.com"), valid password (e.g., "Password123!")
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid email address in the email field.
2. Enter valid password in the password field.
3. Click on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the home screen.

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Password
Preconditions: User has a registered account and is on the Login screen of the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., "user@example.com"), invalid password (e.g., "WrongPassword!")
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid email address in the email field.
2. Enter invalid password in the password field.
3. Click on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating invalid credentials. User remains on the Login screen.

Title: Successful Creation of a Service Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the "Create Announcement" screen.
Test Data: Valid title (e.g., "Need help with gardening"), valid description (e.g., "I need help trimming my bushes"), valid service type (e.g., "Gardening"), valid location (e.g., "Via Roma, 123"), valid date and time (e.g., "2023-12-25 10:00")
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid title in the title field.
2. Enter valid description in the description field.
3. Select valid service type from the dropdown menu.
4. Enter valid location in the location field.
5. Select valid date and time for the service.
6. Click on the "Post Announcement" button.
Expected Result: Announcement is successfully created and displayed in the user's profile and the neighborhood feed.

Title: Creation of Announcement with Missing Required Field
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the "Create Announcement" screen.
Test Data: Missing title, valid description (e.g., "I need help trimming my bushes"), valid service type (e.g., "Gardening"), valid location (e.g., "Via Roma, 123"), valid date and time (e.g., "2023-12-25 10:00")
Test Steps:
1. Leave the title field empty.
2. Enter valid description in the description field.
3. Select valid service type from the dropdown menu.
4. Enter valid location in the location field.
5. Select valid date and time for the service.
6. Click on the "Post Announcement" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating the title field is required. User remains on the "Create Announcement" screen.

Title: Successful Viewing of an Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in and there is at least one announcement posted in the system.
Test Data: Existing announcement (e.g., "Need help with gardening")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the neighborhood feed or search for an existing announcement.
2. Click on the announcement titled "Need help with gardening".
Expected Result: Announcement details (title, description, service type, location, date, and time) are displayed correctly.

Title: Successful Booking of an Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in and there is at least one available announcement.
Test Data: Existing announcement (e.g., "Need help with gardening")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the neighborhood feed and find the announcement titled "Need help with gardening".
2. Click on the "Book" button next to the announcement.
3. Confirm the booking in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: Announcement is marked as booked, and a notification is sent to the service provider. User receives a confirmation message.

Title: Successful Acceptance of a Booking Request
Preconditions: User is logged in as a service provider and has received a booking request for an announcement.
Test Data: Pending booking request for an announcement (e.g., "Need help with gardening")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Booking Requests" section.
2. Find the pending booking request for the announcement titled "Need help with gardening".
3. Click on the "Accept" button next to the request.
Expected Result: Booking request is accepted, and a confirmation message is displayed. Both the user and the service provider receive notifications.

Title: Successful Deletion of an Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in as the owner of an announcement.
Test Data: Existing announcement (e.g., "Need help with gardening")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user's profile or announcement list.
2. Find the announcement titled "Need help with gardening".
3. Click on the "Delete" button next to the announcement.
4. Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: Announcement is successfully deleted, and a confirmation message is displayed. The announcement is no longer visible in the feed or user's profile.

Title: Successful Viewing of Another User's Profile
Preconditions: User is logged in and there is at least one other user in the system.
Test Data: Existing user profile (e.g., "Jane Smith")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the neighborhood feed or search for another user.
2. Click on the profile of "Jane Smith".
Expected Result: User profile details (name, email, phone number, posted announcements, and booking history) are displayed correctly.

Title: Successful Editing of User Account Information
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the account settings screen.
Test Data: Updated name (e.g., "Johnathan Doe"), updated phone number (e.g., "+393987654321")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the account settings.
2. Enter updated name in the name field.
3. Enter updated phone number in the phone number field.
4. Click on the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Account information is successfully updated, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Successful Editing of an Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in as the owner of an announcement and on the announcement details screen.
Test Data: Existing announcement (e.g., "Need help with gardening"), updated description (e.g., "I need help trimming my bushes and mowing the lawn")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the announcement titled "Need help with gardening".
2. Click on the "Edit" button.
3. Enter updated description in the description field.
4. Click on the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Announcement is successfully updated, and a confirmation message is displayed. Updated details are visible in the announcement.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User is not registered in the system
Test Data: Valid email, password, name, and location data
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the registration page.
3. User enters valid email address.
4. User enters valid password.
5. User enters name.
6. User enters location data.
7. User confirms registration by clicking "Sign Up" or equivalent button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and the system redirects the user to the login or home page with a confirmation message.

Title: Registration with Missing Required Field
Preconditions: User is not registered in the system
Test Data: Valid email, empty password field, name, and location data
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the registration page.
3. User enters valid email address.
4. User leaves password field empty.
5. User enters name.
6. User enters location data.
7. User attempts to confirm registration by clicking "Sign Up" or equivalent button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating that the password field is required.

Title: Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User is not registered in the system
Test Data: Invalid email format (e.g., "user@example"), password, name, and location data
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the registration page.
3. User enters invalid email format (e.g., "user@example").
4. User enters valid password.
5. User enters name.
6. User enters location data.
7. User attempts to confirm registration by clicking "Sign Up" or equivalent button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating that the email format is invalid.

Title: Device Geo-localization and Nearby User Identification
Preconditions: User is logged in and has enabled location services
Test Data: User location data (e.g., coordinates of a specific neighborhood)
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. System requests and obtains user location permissions.
3. User grants location access.
4. System displays a map or list of nearby users/devices.
5. User verifies the number of active users in the vicinity.
Expected Result: System accurately identifies and displays nearby users/devices within the selected neighborhood or area.

Title: Geo-localization with Denied Location Permissions
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. System requests location permissions.
3. User denies location access.
4. User attempts to view nearby users or services.
Expected Result: System displays a message indicating that location services are required to access geo-localized features.

Title: Automatic Assignment of Punti Quartiere After Service Completion
Preconditions: User has completed a service as requested in an announcement
Test Data: Service completion confirmation (e.g., user marks task as done)
Test Steps:
1. User completes a service requested in an announcement.
2. User confirms completion of the service in the application.
3. System verifies the completion criteria.
Expected Result: System automatically assigns the predefined number of Punti Quartiere to the user’s account.

Title: Display of Punti Quartiere Balance
Preconditions: User is logged in and has completed at least one service
Test Data: User account with assigned Punti Quartiere
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the profile or wallet section.
3. User views the Punti Quartiere balance.
Expected Result: System displays the correct balance of Punti Quartiere assigned to the user’s account.

Title: Publishing a Service Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in as a "Pubblicatore annuncio"
Test Data: Valid service description, title, and location
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the announcement creation page.
3. User enters a title for the service.
4. User enters a detailed description of the service.
5. User selects the location for the service.
6. User confirms publication by clicking "Post Announcement" or equivalent button.
Expected Result: Announcement is published successfully and appears in the system for other users to view.

Title: Viewing Published Announcements
Preconditions: At least one announcement is published in the system
Test Data: Published service announcements
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the announcements section.
3. User views the list of published announcements.
Expected Result: System displays all published announcements with relevant details (title, description, location, etc.).

Title: New User Registration
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Valid user details (name, email, password)
Test Steps:

2. User fills in the required fields (name, email, password).
3. User submits the registration form.
Expected Result: A new user profile is created successfully.

Title: Successful User Signup
Preconditions: User is not registered in the application

Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Creation of a New Announcement

Title: Manual Deletion of Announcement by Creator
Preconditions: User is logged in and has created an announcement.
Test Data: Valid announcement ID created by the user.
Test Steps:

2. User selects the announcement to delete.
3. User confirms deletion via a confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: The announcement is permanently removed from the bulletin board, and a success notification appears.

Title: Automatic Deletion of Expired Announcement
Preconditions: System time is set to a date beyond the announcement's expiration.
Test Data: Announcement with an expiration date in the past.
Test Steps:
1. System checks for expired announcements.
2. System triggers automatic deletion of the expired announcement.
Expected Result: The announcement is permanently removed from the bulletin board, and no manual action is required.

Title: Automatic Deletion of Assigned Announcement
Preconditions: An announcement has been taken in charge by another user.
Test Data: Announcement marked as "taken in charge."
Test Steps:
1. System detects the announcement is taken in charge.
2. System triggers automatic deletion of the announcement.
Expected Result: The announcement is permanently removed from the bulletin board, and no manual action is required.

Title: View Another User’s Profile
Preconditions: User is logged in and another user’s profile exists.

Test Steps:

2. User selects the profile to view.
Expected Result: The profile details (as defined in the system) are displayed without errors.

Title: Modify User Account Information

3. User confirms changes via a submit button.
Expected Result: The account details are updated successfully, and a confirmation message appears.

Title: Modify Existing Announcement Details

2. User selects the announcement to modify.
3. User edits the announcement details.
4. User confirms changes via a submit button.

Title: Successful Account Creation with Valid Data
Preconditions: User has an internet connection and is on the signup screen
Test Data: Unique email (e.g., testuser123@gmail.com), valid phone number (e.g., +393123456789), name (e.g., John Doe), password (e.g., SecurePass123), valid address (e.g., Via Roma 1, 00100 Roma)
Test Steps:
1. User enters name, surname, phone number, email, address, and password in the signup form.
2. User clicks the "Register" or "Create Account" button.
Expected Result: A success message appears confirming account creation, and user data is stored in Firebase.

Title: Duplicate Email Rejection
Preconditions: User has an internet connection and is on the signup screen. An account with email testuser123@gmail.com already exists.
Test Data: Email (testuser123@gmail.com), valid phone number, name, password, address
Test Steps:
1. User enters email (testuser123@gmail.com) and other valid details.
2. User clicks the "Register" or "Create Account" button.
Expected Result: An error message appears stating the email is already in use.

Title: Duplicate Phone Number Rejection
Preconditions: User has an internet connection and is on the signup screen. An account with phone number +393123456789 already exists.
Test Data: Unique email, phone number (+393123456789), name, password, address
Test Steps:
1. User enters phone number (+393123456789) and other valid details.
2. User clicks the "Register" or "Create Account" button.
Expected Result: An error message appears stating the phone number is already in use.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has created an account and has internet access
Test Data: Valid registered email and correct password
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the registered email in the email field.
3. Enter the correct password in the password field.
4. Click the login button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the main application screen.

Title: View Announcements List and Details
Preconditions: At least one announcement exists in the system created by another user
Test Data: None
Test Steps:

2. Scroll through the list of announcements.
3. Click on any announcement in the list.
Expected Result: The selected announcement details are displayed on the screen.

Title: Reserve an Announcement
Preconditions: User is logged in and an announcement exists
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the announcements list page.
2. Locate an announcement and click the reservation button.
Expected Result: The user is added to the reservation list for the selected announcement.

Title: Accept a Reservation Request
Preconditions: At least one user has reserved the announcement created by the logged-in user
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the reservation list for the announcement.
2. Select one or more users from the reservation list.
3. Click the "Accept" button.
Expected Result: The selected user(s) are accepted for the announcement and added to the list of users who must perform the requested task.

Title: View Another User's Profile
Preconditions: Another user's profile exists in the database
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the announcements list or any page where user profiles are linked.
2. Click on the profile link of another user.
Expected Result: The profile page of the selected user is displayed, showing public information (profile photo, name, email).

Title: Modify User Account Information
Preconditions: User has an existing account
Test Data: New profile photo, updated name, additional personal information
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the account modification section.
2. Upload a new profile photo.
3. Update the name field with new information.
4. Add or remove additional personal information.
5. Click the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: The user's profile is updated with the new information, and changes are saved in the database.

Title: Modify Existing Advertisement
Preconditions: User must have created an advertisement (precondition explicitly stated)
Test Data: An existing advertisement with at least one editable field (e.g., title, description, price)
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the section where their created advertisements are listed.
3. User selects the advertisement to be modified.
4. User clicks the "Edit" or "Modify" button/option.
5. User changes at least one field (e.g., title, description, price).
6. User confirms the changes by clicking "Save" or "Update."
Expected Result: The advertisement is updated in the database, and the changes are reflected in the user's advertisement list.

Title: User Logout and Offline Mode Activation
Preconditions: User must be logged in to the application (precondition explicitly stated)
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User is logged in to the application.
2. User navigates to the logout option (e.g., profile menu, settings, or dedicated logout button).
3. User selects the "Logout" option.
Expected Result: The application closes the session, disconnects the user, and switches to offline mode. The user is no longer able to access logged-in features.

Test Steps:

2. User selects the registration option.
3. User fills in registration details (name, email, password, etc.).
4. User submits the registration form.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and can access the app without significant difficulty.

Title: Verify login functionality for registered users
Preconditions: User is registered in the ShareShelf app
Test Data: Registered user credentials (email and password)
Test Steps:
1. User opens the ShareShelf app.
2. User selects the login option.
3. User enters registered email and password.
4. User submits the login form.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and can access the app features.

Title: Verify manual announcement posting functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in to the ShareShelf app
Test Data: Valid announcement details (title, description, date, time, address)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the announcement posting section.
2. User fills in announcement details (title, description, date, time).

Title: Verify absence of user-to-user communication feature

Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the section where communication with other users might be expected (e.g., announcement details page).
2. User attempts to find a chat or messaging option.
Expected Result: No chat or messaging feature is available for communication between users.

Title: Verify absence of automated recurring announcement feature
Preconditions: User is logged in to the ShareShelf app
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the announcement posting section.
2. User attempts to set an announcement to recur automatically at a specified interval.
Expected Result: No option to set automated recurring announcements is available; user must manually repost each occurrence.

Title: Verify absence of reposting expired announcements feature
Preconditions: User has posted an announcement that has expired due to disavowal or lack of booking
